events.title: Walker Hayes
events.description: Country superstar Walker Hayes is a singer-songwriter from Mobile, Alabama, known for blending country storytelling with pop and hip-hop-inspired production. His breakout smash “Fancy Like” topped country sales and streaming charts, spent over six months at No. 1 on Billboard’s Hot Country Songs chart, reached the Top 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, hit No. 1 at country radio, and earned Hayes his first GRAMMY nomination for Best Country Song. The viral hit was also featured in a nationwide Applebee’s commercial. With additional fan favorites like “AA,” “Y’all Life,” and “U Gurl,” Hayes has become one of the top-selling and most-streamed artists in modern country music. His latest project, 17 Problems, is his most personal work to date—an introspective album shaped by resilience, faith, family, grief, and growth. Featuring both high-energy anthems and deeply emotional storytelling, the album highlights Hayes’ evolution as an artist and songwriter. Known for his authenticity and engaging live performances, Hayes has appeared on Good Morning America, The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, TODAY, and CBS Sunday Morning. Whether delivering chart-topping hits or stripped-down acoustic moments, Walker Hayes invites audiences into a powerful, honest, and unforgettable live experience. Wildwood Get ready for an evening of crowd-pleasing country music with Wildwood, one of Utah’s fastest-rising country acts. Led by powerhouse vocalists Chad Truman and Kate Miller, Wildwood delivers a dynamic blend of modern country hits, classic favorites, and original songs, all brought to life with rich harmonies, exceptional musicianship, and infectious energy. Known for their engaging stage presence and ability to get audiences singing and dancing along, Wildwood has performed throughout the Mountain West, sharing stages with nationally recognized country artists and earning praise for their authentic sound and high-energy performances. events.title: Concerts on the Slopes
events.description: Walker Hayes returns with his most introspective and heartfelt project yet, 17 Problems, an album shaped by reflection, resilience, and raw honesty. Blending hip-hop-infused production with country storytelling, Hayes dives deep into themes of fatherhood, faith, grief, and growth. From the aching vulnerability of “17 Year Old Problems” and “Song for My Son” to the high-energy anthems “Mother” and “Neon Lights,” the album captures the full spectrum of life’s beauty and messiness. With over 100 songs written for this release, Hayes carefully curated 17 tracks that reveal the most personal side of his artistry to date, working with top collaborators like Warren “Oak” Felder, Shane McAnally, and Bart Millard while also penning deeply personal solo material. To bring this story to life, Hayes has announced the Walker Hayes Unplugged Tour, where he offers fans an intimate, acoustic experience unlike anything he’s done before. Stripping back the production to let the lyrics lead, Hayes takes audiences on a journey through the highs and lows that shaped him - from viral fame to quiet family moments, from deep heartbreak to enduring faith. Through heartfelt storytelling and soul-stirring songs, this special evening invites fans into the heart of his story. More than a concert, it’s a celebration of connection, resilience, and the messy, beautiful reality of life, one song at a time....
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from a showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission to the event is free with the purchase of a lift ticket . Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.description: UB40 Featuring Ali Campbell – Live in Concert Get ready for a legendary night of reggae under the stars as UB40 featuring Ali Campbell brings their iconic sound to Concerts on the Slopes in Park City, where Music Meets the Mountains. With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal. Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production. Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes. events.title: A Dog’s Way Home (Kamas)
events.description: As a puppy, Bella finds her way into the arms of Lucas, a young man who gives her a good home. When Bella becomes separated from Lucas, she soon finds herself on an epic 400-mile journey to reunite with her beloved owner. Along the way, the lost but spirited dog touches the lives of an orphaned mountain lion, a down-on-his-luck veteran and some friendly strangers who happen to cross her path. ***Based on the book of the same name by W. Bruce Cameron.*** Join us under the stars in Kamas for a free film screening with free food trucks! Presented with Spanish Subtitles. **Event Details:** Outdoor film screening at the Kamas City Park (505 S Main St, Kamas, UT 84036) Free Food Trucks on site from 5-7pm, movie at 7pm (with Spanish subtitles) Blankets and lawn chairs welcome In partnership with the Kamas Valley Community Foundation, we are accepting donations of non-perishable, unexpired food items to benefit the Kamas and Oakley Food Pantries. ***Presented in partnership with Summit County Health***...

events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description: Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show. Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades. Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today. Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss. Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass). Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.” More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

events.title: Maoli
events.description: Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before. The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas. With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.title: Cory Mon
events.description: ..music, my blessing, my curse, its in me and it must come out i can't help it, i must play, write, compose and perform. Without i am incomplete, while with call me misfit in this world of the incorporated. I am a man confused and divided thus in recourse i am often brightened and enlightened. I emphatically give way to both light and dark, i live in pleasure and pain. my name: cory. Singer/songwriter Cory Mon has a distinctly unique, albeit pleasant voice in the world of music. “Happy \(sometimes sad\), gritty, funky, folksy, bluesy, rocky music… I guess”… he says when asked the dreaded, “So what type of music do you play?” Assuming the life of a musician, Cory has thrived as well as endured through the many ups and downs that have come throughout his well tenured 20\+ year music career. Cory has released 60\+ original songs and toured with and/or supported a handful of his favorite artists: JJ Grey & Mofro, Sean Hayes, The Avett Brothers, Patterson Hood etc. www.corymon.com...

events.description: **Four-part WANDER series focused on women’s hormonal health throughout the seasons (with 8 bonus monthly Zoom calls for monthly check ins)** ***Are fatigue, mood shifts, or hormonal changes dimming your spark?*** You’re not alone. Whether you’re navigating postpartum recovery, fertility planning, perimenopause, postmenopause, or simply wanting more energy and clarity, this series will help you realign with your body’s wisdom and the earth’s cycles. Step into the unique rhythm of each season with Wild Women Tribe’s **Seasonal Hormone Series**, co-hosted by naturopathic physician Dr. Trevor Cates, in a powerful blend of nature, wellness and community. This transformative quarterly series invites you to connect deeply with nature’s cycles through intentional movement, seasonal nourishment, and a sisterhood community of other women who are committed to their natural health journey. events.description: RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. events.title: lake effect: defending the greatest snow on earth panel discussion
events.description: lake effect: defending the greatest snow on earth panel discussion Thursday, August 27th | 6 PM Doors | 6:30 PM Start | Our Lady of the Snows | Free | Sign-up Required Join us for a panel discussion on the relationship between the Great Salt Lake and snowpack in the Wasatch Mountains. Presented by ACE, Torrey House Press, Grow the Flow, and Save Our Canyons, LAKE EFFECT is a call to understand the connection between the Great Salt Lake and the Wasatch Mountains and how we can defend the greatest snow on earth. Featuring Ayja Bounous and Teri Harman, writers and contributors to the anthology The Once and Future Lake: Stories for Great Salt Lake; Jake Dreyfous, managing director of Grow the Flow; and Jack Stauss, Executive Director of Save Our Canyons; and moderated by Torrey House Press Executive Director Kirsten Johanna Allen, LAKE EFFECT invites a community discussion on how to maintain a legacy of winter for future generations. ABOUT THE PANELISTS: Kirsten Johanna Allen \(she/her\) directs acquisitions, editing, production, and marketing for Torrey House Press with co-executive director Will Neville-Rehbehn. As co-founder, Kirsten has steered the press from its earliest inklings to publish conservation through literature to become the powerful platform for books and ideas about the natural world and the West that it is today. Kirsten holds a BA from Westminster College and an MPH from the University of Utah. She has two grown children and lives with a pair of cats and her spouse, Mark Bailey, in Salt Lake City and Torrey, Utah. Ayja Bounous is a Salt Lake City-based writer inspired by water in all its forms. She is the award-winning author of Junior Bounous and the Joys of Skiing and Shaped by Snow: Defending the Future of Winter, and a contributor to Alta and Little Cottonwood: Jewel of the Wasatch Mountains and The Once and Future Lake: Stories for Great Salt Lake. Ayja holds a master's in Environmental Humanities from the University of Utah and hosts Snow \+...

events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.

events.title: MAX
events.description: MAX and Marc E. Bassy come together for an unforgettable night of genre-blending pop, R&B, and high-energy live performance. Multi-platinum artist MAX leads the night with his powerhouse vocals, infectious pop sound, and global hits like “Lights Down Low” and music from his latest album, LOVE IN STEREO, which have earned over 3 billion streams worldwide. Known for his dynamic stage presence and emotional delivery, MAX brings a live show full of energy, connection, and sing-along moments. Special Guest - OpenerJoining him is Bay Area artist Marc E. Bassy, celebrated for his soulful vocals, genre-defying style, and hit “You & Me” (feat. G-Eazy). With a career built on authenticity and creative independence, Marc delivers a performance that feels both intimate and electrifying.Together, they bring powerful vocals, emotional storytelling, and undeniable chemistry to the stage—creating a dynamic live experience you won’t want to miss.

events.description: Get ready to throw it back to the ultimate decade of music, style, and unforgettable party anthems as I Love the 90s takes over Concerts on the Slopes on Friday, September 4! Featuring legendary hitmakers Vanilla Ice, C&C Music Factory and Color Me Badd, this high-energy night will transport fans straight back to the golden era of pop, hip hop, and R&B. From the chart-topping smash “Ice Ice Baby” to the smooth harmonies of “I Wanna Sex You Up” and “All 4 Love,” this is the ultimate summer celebration of 90s nostalgia. Bring your friends, sing along to the songs you grew up with, and dance under the stars at the beautiful Canyons Village in Park City. DJ Proof will open the night to get the party started! events.title: Labor Day Experiences
events.description: Celebrate the close of summer with a long weekend designed for easygoing fun, outdoor play, and family-friendly moments. From lively daytime activities to relaxed evening experiences, Labor Day at Deer Valley offers the perfect setting to soak in the season’s final days. **DALY’S DINING & GAMES** ***Daly’s Pub & Rec*** **September 4 – 6 | 2:00pm – 9:00pm** Relax and recharge at Daly’s Pub & Rec, where casual dining meets interactive entertainment. Open throughout the afternoon and evening, Daly’s offers approachable fare, handcrafted beverages, bowling lanes, and arcade games, making it an easygoing destination to gather and enjoy the holiday weekend. **ANIMAL SHOW** ***Compass Sports*** **September 4 | 3:00pm** Meet fascinating animals native to Utah and hear engaging stories from their caretakers during this interactive wildlife presentation. Designed to educate and entertain, the animal show offers a memorable experience for guests of all ages. **CANDY LAND** ***5th Floor*** **September 4 – 6 | 3:00pm – 4:30pm** Children are invited to explore a curated candy station and select their favorite sweets to create a personalized assortment. A simple, playful activity designed especially for younger guests, Candy Land offers a cheerful way to enjoy a sweet moment during Labor Day weekend. **CHILDREN’S NIGHT OUT** ***Paintbox*** **September 4 & 5 | 5:00pm – 9:00pm** While parents enjoy an evening during Labor Day weekend, younger guests are invited to spend the night at Paintbox for a fun and thoughtfully curated experience. Children’s Night Out features engaging craft projects, a family-friendly movie, and a relaxed dinner, all guided by Montage Deer Valley’s trained team. Designed to feel playful, comfortable, and welcoming, the evening offers children a memorable night of creativity and connection while parents enjoy time of their own. *Reservations required.* **VEUVE CLICQUOT APRÈS LOUNGE: SUNSET SERIES** ***Après Lounge*** **September 4 & 5 ...
events.location: Montage Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description: Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him. Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next. The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge. Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: create your own stacker rings
events.description: create your own stacker rings Thursday, September 10th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $25 | Sign-up Required In this hands-on workshop, you will have the opportunity to create TWO of your own stacker rings \(one sterling silver, one gold-filled\). You will learn how to measure, file, and solder your stackers. By the end of class, you'll leave with beautiful, handcrafted stackers you'll be proud to wear, plus the foundational metalsmithing skills to continue building your craft. No prior experience is necessary. You do not need to know your ring size prior. For ages 18 and up. With over 5 years of metalsmithing experience and a teaching background, teaching jewelry making is Megan's passion. She also teaches jewelry making at Salt Lake Community College and runs a small jewelry business, Crooked Trails Silver . Megan's teaching philosophy blends patience, creativity, and encouragement - whether you're a complete beginner or expanding your skills. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kids Trail Race
events.description: Join us for **Moose on the Loose 2026,** a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are **June 13**, **July 18**, **August 15**, and **September 12**, with all races beginning at **9:00 AM**. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.description: ACE men's group Thursday, September 17th | 5:30 PM | Alta Community Center | Free The ACE Men's Group purpose is to provide personal and professional support to men connected to the Alta and Little Cottonwood communities. The power in groups lies in the unique opportunity to give and receive multiple perspectives, support, encouragement and feedback to and from other individuals in a safe and confidential environment. The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. His practice focuses on supporting individuals to develop insight into themselves, plan for making positive change and execution of those plans to reach their goals. Daniel is an lover of the outdoors, especially Alta and all it has to offer. Following meetings of each month: 6/18 7/16 8/20 9/17 10/15 ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all our attendees. Please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Monster Drawing Rally
events.description: About Monster Drawing Rally is a live art-making fundraiser with local artists, benefiting the Arts Council of Park City & Summit County and Kimball Art Center. Monster Drawing Rally is a fundraising event benefiting the Arts Council of Park City & Summit County and Kimball Art Center. Held outdoors at Kimball Art Center, the event features live music, food, a cash bar, and live art-making sessions where artists create pieces in 50 minutes. Spectators can watch the creative process, build relationships with local artists, and purchase finished artwork on the spot. The event includes various mediums such as graphite, ink, charcoal, painting, digital art, and collage. If more than one person is interested in a piece, a random card draw will decide the buyer. Tickets are $25 for adults, $10 for kids aged 6-17, and free for children 5 and under. Get tickets. Be part of Monster as a volunteer! Click here to see all available positions. Are you an interested artist? Arts Council of Park City & Summit County's website has all application details....
events.location: Kimball Art Center, Park City, UT

events.description: Save the Date for Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 National Day of Service and Remembrance. The day starts at 8 a.m. in City Park with a complimentary breakfast for all volunteers, followed by a short program that honors 9/11/01. At 9 a.m. all volunteers head to their Serve Park City service projects they have signed up for on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity that are being held on 9/19/26. (These projects will be posted by August 1, 2026) Last year we had 35 different projects all over the Park City area that ran from 9 a.m. -12 noon with over 1200 volunteers participating. We invite all to serve. This event is hosted by Serve Park City, Inc. A nonprofit whose mission is to build community through service. Each year we partner with over 30 nonprofits and organizations in hosting a day of service, but find opportunities to serve throughout the year on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity. events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: ACE men's group
events.description: ACE men's group Thursday, October 15th | 5:30 PM | Alta Community Center | Free The ACE Men's Group purpose is to provide personal and professional support to men connected to the Alta and Little Cottonwood communities. The power in groups lies in the unique opportunity to give and receive multiple perspectives, support, encouragement and feedback to and from other individuals in a safe and confidential environment. The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. His practice focuses on supporting individuals to develop insight into themselves, plan for making positive change and execution of those plans to reach their goals. Daniel is an lover of the outdoors, especially Alta and all it has to offer. Following meetings of each month: 6/18 7/16 8/20 9/17 10/15 ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all our attendees. Please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Alta Community Center 10351 Utah 210, Alta, UT

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, October 22nd | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Miles Glynn: Westernish
events.description: Join us October 23rd to kick off our first show of the fall, and experience new works by Miles Glynn at Gallery MAR, Park City. Miles Glynn grew up seeing the American West through a car window, the son of an Army photojournalist, trained from childhood to see the world in frames and compositions. That eye never left him. Working from Marfa, Texas, Glynn builds layered mixed-media paintings from vintage photographs, forgotten advertising, and Western ephemera, combining silkscreen, paint, and collage into works that feel at once deeply familiar and entirely new. The textures of deteriorating buildings, weathered billboards, and desert landscapes find their way into every surface, documents of time, resilience, and the stories that linger after everything else fades. His work doesn't ask you to see what he sees. It asks you to find what only you would....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Geoffrey Gersten: New Works
events.description: Geoffrey Gersten's large-scale oil paintings turn the forgotten objects of mid-20th-century life into something unmistakably alive, join us November 27th for his Gallery MAR debut. We're proud to welcome Geoffrey Gersten to Gallery MAR for his first show with us. His studio smells like oil paint and nostalgia. Matchbooks, bottle caps, vintage toys, and torn advertising ephemera line every surface, remnants of a mid-20th-century world that most people have forgotten and Gersten refuses to let go. Working in oil on large-format canvases, he elevates these once-ordinary objects to something close to monumental, rendering them with a precision that borders on photorealistic. Step forward and the paint asserts itself as unmistakably made by hand. It's a tightrope Gersten walks with confidence: classical technique in service of deeply personal subject matter. “New Works” brings together his latest paintings, each one a celebration of life's timeless pleasures and treasures, rendered with an ever-fresh spirit that makes the past feel startlingly alive. Join us November 27th from 6–9 PM to experience new works by Geoffrey Gersten at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: salt lake ballet co-op with the lingo
events.description: salt lake ballet co-op with the lingo Sunday, August 9th | 5 PM Doors | 5:30 PM Ballet | Snowpine Lodge Grand Lawn | $10 | Tickets Required | Kids Under 5 are Free Salt Lake Ballet Cooperative, a professional-level movement cooperative, is a program of Deseret Experimental Opera Company \(DEXO\). Founded in 2013, DEXO has produced many premier works; incorporating both traditional and non-traditional approaches. By bringing together diverse specialists ranging from musicians to dancers to writers to actors, we strive for the age-old goal of opera: a work greater than the sum of its parts. This summer, Salt Lake Ballet Cooperative \(SLBC\) is thrilled to be partnering with local band, The Lingo. Lead vocalist Adam Borgoine, bassist Alex Millet, keyboardist Joe Pederson, guitarist Sam Morrison, and drummer Bridger Croxford make up this groovy psych-rock band. Their influence ranges from prototype punk to surf-rock from the 60s. Over the past several years, SLBC, a program of Deseret Experimental Opera, has cultivated partnerships with local musicians and bands to present original, unique performances. You are in for a truly one-of-a-kind experience with this all-new collaboration. One thing is for sure, this show promises to bring the groove! FACTS & TIPS: This event will take place on the Grand Lawn of the Snowpine Lodge You must bring your own blanket or LOW chairs \(must be 31.5" height or less\). Food and beverages available for purchase Outside food and beverages NOT allowed 5 PM Doors - 5:30pm Show begins ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...

events.title: Live Music at Silver Lake
events.description: Food Trucks, Music & Scenic Adventures — Join us Friday through Sunday on Silver Lake Lawn for live music, local food trucks, and relaxed mountain afternoons. Settle into a lounge chair, enjoy a cold drink, and take in alpine views while a rotating lineup of artists fills the air with acoustic rock, country, blues, and more. With hiking trails just steps away, it’s an easy, all-day experience for everyone. BUY LIFT TICKETS THIS WEEKEND’S LINEUP Friday, August 7 Cry Wolf Saturday, August 8 Pompe n Honey Sunday, August 9 Vorraro
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from a showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission to the event is free with the purchase of a lift ticket . Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Roméo et Juliette
events.description: You've read the play - maybe seen the movie, the musical, e ven the ballet. But have you experienced the opera?August 21 at 7:00 PM & August 23 at 3:00 PM Eccles Center, Park City In Charles Gounod's Roméo et Juliette, Shakespeare's star-crossed lovers come to life amid the pageantry and passion of Verona, reimagined through the grand orchestral and theatrical language of French Romantic opera. Arrive early to explore displays of art, history, and themed concessions that set the scene for Roméo et Juliette, and stay after for a Q&A with the artists and creative team. A 40-player orchestra and soaring voices deliver the full grandeur of opera in an immersive experience close to home....

events.title: Labor Day Experiences
events.description: Celebrate the close of summer with a long weekend designed for easygoing fun, outdoor play, and family-friendly moments. From lively daytime activities to relaxed evening experiences, Labor Day at Deer Valley offers the perfect setting to soak in the season’s final days. **DALY’S DINING & GAMES** ***Daly’s Pub & Rec*** **September 4 – 6 | 2:00pm – 9:00pm** Relax and recharge at Daly’s Pub & Rec, where casual dining meets interactive entertainment. Open throughout the afternoon and evening, Daly’s offers approachable fare, handcrafted beverages, bowling lanes, and arcade games, making it an easygoing destination to gather and enjoy the holiday weekend. **ANIMAL SHOW** ***Compass Sports*** **September 4 | 3:00pm** Meet fascinating animals native to Utah and hear engaging stories from their caretakers during this interactive wildlife presentation. Designed to educate and entertain, the animal show offers a memorable experience for guests of all ages. **CANDY LAND** ***5th Floor*** **September 4 – 6 | 3:00pm – 4:30pm** Children are invited to explore a curated candy station and select their favorite sweets to create a personalized assortment. A simple, playful activity designed especially for younger guests, Candy Land offers a cheerful way to enjoy a sweet moment during Labor Day weekend. **CHILDREN’S NIGHT OUT** ***Paintbox*** **September 4 & 5 | 5:00pm – 9:00pm** While parents enjoy an evening during Labor Day weekend, younger guests are invited to spend the night at Paintbox for a fun and thoughtfully curated experience. Children’s Night Out features engaging craft projects, a family-friendly movie, and a relaxed dinner, all guided by Montage Deer Valley’s trained team. Designed to feel playful, comfortable, and welcoming, the evening offers children a memorable night of creativity and connection while parents enjoy time of their own. *Reservations required.* **VEUVE CLICQUOT APRÈS LOUNGE: SUNSET SERIES** ***Après Lounge*** **September 4 & 5 ...
events.location: Montage Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...

events.description: Eric Heideman is a 27-year-old blues musician living in Austin, Texas, from Salt Lake City, Utah. Heideman was personally requested to open shows for ZZ Top at Eccles Theatre and Walter Trout and Mike Zito at The State Room. He was a featured performer at the Utah Blues Fest in 2022, 2023, & 2024. He was a featured performer at SXSW 2025 and the 2025 Durango Blues Train. He recorded his latest album, “Third Degree Gravity” with Grammy Nominated Victor Wainwright and International Blues Challenge winner JW-Jones.

events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

With hiking trails just steps away, it’s an easy, all-day experience for everyone. events.title: Guided Historical Hike
events.description: Discover Park City's mining history — Step into Park City’s past on a guided hike through Deer Valley’s scenic terrain, where fresh mountain air meets stories of the area’s rich mining history. This three- to four-hour hike covers moderate terrain, typically under six miles, as guests hike around Empire. Meet at the Mid-Mountain Trailhead on Marsac Ave, above the Montage, for an 8:30 a.m. departure.

Love & Special Sauce on Sunday, along with scenic chairlift access throughout the resort, outdoor dining, and family-friendly activities. Sample a curated selection of Utah’s finest craft beers, including full-strength varieties, and discover diverse styles from beloved local breweries. Please note: festival access is only available via a chairlift ride on Silver Lake Express, with parking and check-in at Snow Park Lodge. Tickets go on sale July 10 at 10 a.m. MT.

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: fearless abstract painting
events.description: fearless abstract painting Tuesday , August 18th | 6PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $15 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to create fearless, intuitive abstract paintings using Samantha da Silva's signature "no rules" roller-painting method, incorporating plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and playful canvas manipulation techniques. This class is especially good for people who want to learn how to silence their inner critic, overcome fear of the blank canvas, trust their intuition, and discover their own authentic artistic voice in a joyful, judgment-free space. You will use rollers \(instead of brushes\), large quantities of acrylic paint and water, plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and your hands directly on the canvas laid flat on a table. At the end of the class, you will leave with one completed, textured abstract painting, a toolbox of fearless techniques you can use forever, and the confidence to keep creating without rules or self-doubt. Samantha da Silva \(b. 1978, Brazil\) is a Brazilian-born, Salt Lake City-based artist and dedicated educator who has taught visual arts for over twenty years in public and private schools and institutions across Canada, the United States, Hawaii, and France. Shaped by nearly forty moves triggered by natural disasters and life circumstances, and by the intense assimilation she experienced at age ten, da Silva channels themes of migration, belonging, and resourcefulness into her widely recognized large-scale relief sculptures-topographic-like works built from found dirt, sawdust, and debris-that have appeared in Architectural Digest, Bravo TV, and HGTV. In the classroom, she guides students of all ages to transform personal experience and everyday materials into powerful expression, making art a vital tool for connection and resilience in an ever-shifting world. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions...
events.location: Alta Peruvian Lawn 10000 Little Cottonwood Canyon, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Alta Peruvian Lawn 10000 Little Cottonwood Canyon

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” where sweet memories offer fleeting comfort after a lover’s betrayal. Famously called “the apotheosis of the dance itself” by Wagner, Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7 brings the night to a triumphant close. An instant symphonic favorite—with early audiences reportedly demanding a repeat of the second movement—the piece surges with wildness and joy, culminating in one of the most electrifying finales in classical music....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Lunch and Learn
events.description: Join the Park City // Summit County Green Business Program on Wednesday, August 12 at the Park City Chamber from 12 pm to 1 pm to discover the role renewable energy can play as one of many strategies businesses can use to understand, reduce, and transform the energy used in their day-to-day operations. Join experts from Vote Solar**,** Summit County, and U-Crew, as they discuss the benefits, share success stories, and highlight ways businesses can become more involved in clean and renewable energy initiatives. During the session, speakers will discuss the different approaches businesses can take to meet their energy goals, explain the differences between renewable and clean energy, address common energy-related challenges, and highlight current and upcoming Rocky Mountain Power programs. Lunch is provided! Featured Speakers include: Creed McCord, Program Engineer at U-Crew, will share a local program available to businesses seeking to better understand and reduce their energy use. Kate Bowman, Regulatory Director at Vote Solar, will discuss the role renewable energy can play in helping businesses achieve their sustainability goals. Emily Quinton, Summit County Sustainability Director and Secretary of the Utah Renewable Communities Board, will provide the big-picture framework for business energy decision-making. She will introduce the energy hierarchy, explain the differences between clean and renewable energy, and discuss the range of utility-scale energy options available to commercial customers. Emily will also provide an overview of the new Community Clean Energy Program, adopted by Summit County, Park City, Francis, and Oakley, and what it means for local businesses and the future of clean energy in Utah. Register at parkcitygreen.org/events...
events.location: Park City Chamber of Commerce & Visitors Bureau, Park City, UT
events.venue_name: Park City Chamber of Commerce & Visitors Bureau

events.title: Wasatch Trail Run Series
events.description: Lace up your shoes and get running at the Wasatch Trail Run Series. Whether you’re competing for the podium or just looking for an excuse to cruise mountain trails with a great crew, this laid-back race series delivers great views, good vibes and a rewarding challenge. Choose between the short and long course, each of which winds through steep dirt roads and single-track trails across the resort. After the finish line, stick around for the lively post-race scene with activities, giveaways and more. Visit the Wasatch Trail Run website for full details and register for the full season or individual races. Race Details: Long Course | 7.9 miles Start Time: 6:30 pm Elevation Gain: 1800\+ ft Start/Finish: Creekside, Entry 1 Short Course | 5.2 miles Start Time: 6:45 pm Elevation Gain: 850\+ ft Start/Finish: Creekside, Entry 1...

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Opera Around the World: Great Britain — Park City Opera
events.description: Paris is calling - and you don't need a plane ticket! Romantic, shimmering, and utterly enchanting, this evening features excerpts from PCO's upcoming performances of Roméo et Juliette https://www.parkcityopera.org/romeo-et-juliette - because nobody does passion quite like the French. Set against Park City's stunning mountain backdrop, we invite you to bring a picnic, gather your loved ones \(dogs included\), and enjoy opera and vocal chamber music in an open-air, relaxed community setting. This event is free and no tickets are required.

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Last Friday Gallery Stroll
events.description: Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations. Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT

events.title: the art of arrangement
events.description: the art of arrangement Tuesday, August 25th | 6 PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $10 | Sign-up Required From Simple to Stunning! In this class, students will learn how to transform an ordinary store-bought bouquet into a stunning flower arrangement using floral design techniques. By applying principles such as balance, color harmony, proportion, and texture, simple flowers can be arranged into a creative and eye-catching display. Students will practice trimming stems, layering flowers, and adding greenery to create depth and movement, turning a basic bouquet into an elegant centerpiece. This event is for 10 and up. Monika was born and raised in Romania before moving to the United States to follow her passion for art and design. Over the years, she discovered a love for floral design and began creating beautiful arrangements that combine creativity and natural beauty. Four years ago, Monica moved to Utah, where she enjoys the stunning outdoor scenery and the welcoming people who make the state feel like home. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: stamp carving
events.description: stamp carving Tuesday, September 15th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to design, carve, and print with their own handmade rubber stamps. This class is especially good for people who want to learn the basics of stamp carving and printmaking. You will use carving tools, rubber carving blocks, inks, and printing materials during this class. At the end of the class, you will leave with a hand-carved stamp and the skills to continue creating your own custom designs at home. This event is for 9 and up. Melissa Mott is co-owner of Handcraft Revival Folk School & Market in Salt Lake City. She is passionate about preserving and sharing traditional handcraft skills and creating opportunities for people of all ages to learn through making. Over the years, she has taught a wide variety of classes, camps, and community programs focused on creativity, craftsmanship, and hands-on learning. Melissa believes that making things by hand helps us slow down, build confidence, and connect more deeply with both our work and one another. events.title: Military Appreciation Week
events.description: Join us at Snowbird for Military Appreciation Week. Active and retired Military and their immediate family will receive a FREE All-Day Summer Scenic Tram Ride pass to the top of Hidden Peak. How to Redeem Your Tickets: 1. Visit http://bit.ly/4n7RLQu, exclusively for Military members. Select the date and number of additional passes you would like to redeem. Add them to your Snowbird cart and checkout. 2. Assign your tickets and select whether each person needs a ticket card. 3. Fill out the Sheer ID verification with your information \(https://services.sheerid.com/verify/6407ade40bd23a3ee0613db1/\). 4. Once your military status is verified, input the code you receive into the box on the checkout page. 5. Finish the checkout process. Afterwards, you will receive a confirmation email with a QR code you can use to print your passes at one of the many Pick-Up Boxes located throughout the resort....

events.title: gesture drawing
events.description: gesture drawing Tuesday, October 13th | 6PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn to apply the principles of gesture drawing to still-life objects and the human figure. Gesture drawing is fast-paced and a great lesson in learning to be free. This event is for 10 and up. Cat is a local maker and small business owner in the Salt Lake Valley. Art has always been an essential part of Cat's life, through childhood and now. She had the pleasure of attending the University of Tennessee, Knoxville to earn her MS in Teacher Education and BFA in Printmaking and Drawing, with a minor in Art History. She was an art teacher for 8 years, stepping away in 2020 to pursue Honeysuckle Handmade Studio as her full-time job. events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, January 14th | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated, and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones, and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Last Friday Gallery Stroll
events.description: Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations. Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT
